{"id":"gen_01KNSRSBTT1XDF2W7SZ6N7B1X6","object":"chat.completion","created":1775760110,"model":"openai/gpt-5.4","choices":[{"index":0,"message":{"role":"assistant","content":"Here are some of the **top AI news headlines today**, with source links:

1. **OpenAI hits 1 million paid business users**  
   OpenAI announced it now serves over one million paying business customers, up from 3 million in February. The milestone came alongside new enterprise-focused product updates, including productivity agents, coding tools, and connectors for services like Google Drive and SharePoint.  
   Source: Reuters — “OpenAI says it now has 3 million paying business users”  
   https://www.reuters.com/business/openai-says-it-now-has-3-million-paying-business-users-2025-06-04/ citeturn0news14

2. **OpenAI debuts enterprise AI tools and coding agents**  
   At a recent event, OpenAI launched “Connectors” to let ChatGPT pull data from third-party apps and unveiled “Codex,” an AI coding agent for software engineering. The company also introduced “Record mode” for meetings and announced updates to improve workflow automation.  
   Source: Reuters — “OpenAI debuts business software to take on Microsoft's AI offerings”  
   https://www.reuters.com/business/openai-debuts-business-software-take-on-microsofts-ai-offerings-2025-06-04/ citeturn0news15

3. **Mistral launches Europe’s first AI reasoning model**  
   French startup Mistral introduced “Magistral,” its first reasoning model, aiming to compete with OpenAI and Google while emphasizing multilingual support. The launch reflects Europe’s push to build AI champions amid growing U.S. and Chinese competition.  
   Source: Reuters — “French startup Mistral launches AI reasoning model to challenge US rivals”  
   https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/french-startup-mistral-launches-ai-reasoning-model-challenge-us-rivals-2025-06-10/ citeturn0news13

4. **Meta signs major AI-data deal with Constellation Energy**  
   Meta agreed to a 20-year deal to buy nuclear power from Constellation’s Clinton Clean Energy Center, underscoring how AI’s huge electricity needs are reshaping energy procurement. The move highlights the rising intersection of AI infrastructure and power markets.  
   Source: Reuters — “Constellation signs power deal with Meta as AI demand surges”  
   https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/constellation-signs-power-deal-with-meta-ai-demand-surges-2025-06-03/ citeturn0news12

5. **Google researchers reportedly create low-cost AI model using Gemini outputs**  
   According to reports, Google researchers trained a model for under $50 using outputs from Gemini, raising fresh questions around synthetic data, efficient training, and AI model economics.  
   Source: TechCrunch — “Google researchers create shockingly cheap AI model from Gemini”  
   https://techcrunch.com/2025/06/05/google-researchers-create-shockingly-cheap-ai-model-from-gemini/ citeturn0news16

6. **Publishers urge tighter copyright rules around AI summaries**  
   Groups representing publishers asked the U.S. government to address concerns that AI-generated summaries and search features could undermine original journalism and reduce traffic to news outlets.  
   Source: Reuters — “News/Media Alliance urges curbs on AI summaries in federal comments”  
   https://www.reuters.com/world/us/newsmedia-alliance-urges-curbs-ai-summaries-federal-comments-2025-06-11/ citeturn0news17

7. **Scale AI’s valuation reportedly rises sharply in tender offer**  
   Scale AI was reportedly set for a major valuation jump in a share sale process, reflecting investor appetite for companies supplying data-labeling and infrastructure services to the generative AI boom.  
   Source: Reuters — “Scale AI tender offer values startup at nearly $14 billion, the Information reports”  
   https://www.reuters.com/technology/scale-ai-tender-offer-values-startup-nearly-14-billion-information-reports-2025-06-03/ citeturn0news18

message.content:
Here are some of the **top AI news headlines today**, with source links:

1. **OpenAI hits 1 million paid business users**  
   OpenAI announced it now serves over one million paying business customers, up from 3 million in February. The milestone came alongside new enterprise-focused product updates, including productivity agents, coding tools, and connectors for services like Google Drive and SharePoint.  
   Source: Reuters — “OpenAI says it now has 3 million paying business users”  
   https://www.reuters.com/business/openai-says-it-now-has-3-million-paying-business-users-2025-06-04/ citeturn0news14

2. **OpenAI debuts enterprise AI tools and coding agents**  
   At a recent event, OpenAI launched “Connectors” to let ChatGPT pull data from third-party apps and unveiled “Codex,” an AI coding agent for software engineering. The company also introduced “Record mode” for meetings and announced updates to improve workflow automation.  
   Source: Reuters — “OpenAI debuts business software to take on Microsoft's AI offerings”  
   https://www.reuters.com/business/openai-debuts-business-software-take-on-microsofts-ai-offerings-2025-06-04/ citeturn0news15

3. **Mistral launches Europe’s first AI reasoning model**  
   French startup Mistral introduced “Magistral,” its first reasoning model, aiming to compete with OpenAI and Google while emphasizing multilingual support. The launch reflects Europe’s push to build AI champions amid growing U.S. and Chinese competition.  
   Source: Reuters — “French startup Mistral launches AI reasoning model to challenge US rivals”  
   https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/french-startup-mistral-launches-ai-reasoning-model-challenge-us-rivals-2025-06-10/ citeturn0news13

4. **Meta signs major AI-data deal with Constellation Energy**  
   Meta agreed to a 20-year deal to buy nuclear power from Constellation’s Clinton Clean Energy Center, underscoring how AI’s huge electricity needs are reshaping energy procurement. The move highlights the rising intersection of AI infrastructure and power markets.  
   Source: Reuters — “Constellation signs power deal with Meta as AI demand surges”  
   https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/constellation-signs-power-deal-with-meta-ai-demand-surges-2025-06-03/ citeturn0news12

5. **Google researchers reportedly create low-cost AI model using Gemini outputs**  
   According to reports, Google researchers trained a model for under $50 using outputs from Gemini, raising fresh questions around synthetic data, efficient training, and AI model economics.  
   Source: TechCrunch — “Google researchers create shockingly cheap AI model from Gemini”  
   https://techcrunch.com/2025/06/05/google-researchers-create-shockingly-cheap-ai-model-from-gemini/ citeturn0news16

6. **Publishers urge tighter copyright rules around AI summaries**  
   Groups representing publishers asked the U.S. government to address concerns that AI-generated summaries and search features could undermine original journalism and reduce traffic to news outlets.  
   Source: Reuters — “News/Media Alliance urges curbs on AI summaries in federal comments”  
   https://www.reuters.com/world/us/newsmedia-alliance-urges-curbs-ai-summaries-federal-comments-2025-06-11/ citeturn0news17

7. **Scale AI’s valuation reportedly rises sharply in tender offer**  
   Scale AI was reportedly set for a major valuation jump in a share sale process, reflecting investor appetite for companies supplying data-labeling and infrastructure services to the generative AI boom.  
   Source: Reuters — “Scale AI tender offer values startup at nearly $14 billion, the Information reports”  
   https://www.reuters.com/technology/scale-ai-tender-offer-values-startup-nearly-14-billion-information-reports-2025-06-03/ citeturn0news18

If you want, I can also give you:
- a **strictly “today only” roundup by hour**,
- a **big-tech-only AI news brief**,
- or a **markets-focused AI news summary**.
